data.title: Telecom Italia S.p.A. reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Telecom Italia S.p.A. press release (OTCPK:TIIAY): Q1 Group total revenues amounted to 3.3 billion euros, up by 2.7% year-on-year (+1.6% in domestic to 2.2 billion euros, +4.9% in Brazil to 1.0 billion euros); Group service revenues rose by 3.3% year-on-year to 3.1 billion euros (+2.1% in domestic to 2.1 billion euros, +5.6% in Brazil to 1.0 billion euros).
  • Group EBITDA increased, up by 5.7% year-on-year to 1.0 billion euros (+4.1% in domestic to 0.5 billion euros, +6.8% in Brazil to 0.5 billion euros);
  • Group EBITDA After Lease grew sharply, rising 5.4% year-on-year to 0.8 billion euros (+4.0% in domestic to 0.4 billion euros, +6.5% in Brazil to 0.4 billion euros)

data.title: FTAI Infrastructure anticipates $400M EBITDA potential in 2025 with strong growth across segments
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: FTAI Infrastructure Inc. (FIP) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Ken Nicholson announced that the board authorized a quarterly dividend of $0.03 per share, payable on May 27, 2025. He highlighted the continued progress in financial and strategic objectives, supported by the appointment of Buck Fletcher as CFO.
  • Adjusted EBITDA for Q1 2025 was $35.2 million, up 21% from the previous quarter and 29% year-over-year. The company recorded a non-cash gain of $120 million related to the acquisition of the remaining interest in Long Ridge.
  • Nicholson emphasized the company’s line of sight on $190 million of incremental locked-in annual EBITDA and remains optimistic about reaching $400 million in annual EBITDA, excluding potential new investments.
  • CFO Buck Fletcher outlined the company’s refinancing plans, including refinancing corporate bonds and preferred stock to reduce fixed charges and improve cash flow.

Outlook

  • Long Ridge is projected to reach an annualized EBITDA run rate of $160 million by mid-2025, with an additional $30 million in capacity revenue starting June 1, 2025. Data center developments at Long Ridge are estimated to contribute incremental EBITDA of approximately $70 million annually.
  • Jefferson expects $25 million in long-term annual EBITDA from new contracts commencing in 2025, and ongoing negotiations may bring further opportunities.
  • Repauno’s Phase 2 project is expected to generate $80 million of annual EBITDA upon completion in late 2026. Additional capacity from Phase 3 could increase EBITDA by $100 million.
  • Transtar targets annual EBITDA of at least $10 million from new third-party opportunities, with a focus on acquiring complementary railroads to diversify revenue streams.

Financial Results

  • Transtar achieved Q1 adjusted EBITDA of $19.9 million, with stable volumes and costs. It has identified over a dozen new revenue opportunities.
  • Long Ridge reported EBITDA of $18.1 million for Q1 2025, compared to $9.9 million in Q4 2024. March alone generated over $10 million in EBITDA, indicating a strong run rate.
  • Jefferson posted $8 million in adjusted EBITDA, impacted by four storage tanks being off lease. Contracts initiated on April 1 will add $25 million in annual EBITDA.
  • Repauno signed additional letters of intent for Phase 2, securing contracted volumes of 70,000 barrels per day and raising expected annual EBITDA to $80 million.

Q&A

  • Giuliano Bologna, Compass Point: Asked about the timeline for Repauno cavern approvals. CEO Nicholson stated it is expected within 30-45 days post-hearing.
  • Bologna inquired about data center deals at Long Ridge. Nicholson explained these could involve leasing land and providing backup power, potentially adding $70 million in annual EBITDA.
  • Brian McKenna, Citizens: Questioned the impact of tariffs on business. Nicholson highlighted potential benefits for Repauno and Jefferson, with increased interest from European markets.
  • McKenna also asked about the 20 MW Long Ridge power plant upgrade. Nicholson confirmed expected approval in late 2025, contributing $8 million in incremental EBITDA.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised detailed queries about growth opportunities, indicating a positive long-term outlook but cautious over execution risks.
  • Management showed confidence in their initiatives, particularly in Long Ridge and Repauno developments, and emphasized strategic flexibility and financial discipline.
  • Compared to the prior quarter, management’s tone displayed increased optimism due to significant progress in Long Ridge consolidation and data center negotiations.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Adjusted EBITDA rose 21% sequentially, driven by Long Ridge’s consolidation and increased performance.
  • Guidance for 2025 improved, with higher expectations for Long Ridge, Repauno, and Jefferson’s contributions.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted from general concerns to specific growth projects, reflecting confidence in the company’s execution.
  • Management’s tone was more assertive, highlighting recent achievements and future potential.

Risks and Concerns

  • Challenges include global trade uncertainties impacting Transtar and tariff-related impacts.
  • Execution risks in Repauno’s Phase 2 and Long Ridge’s data center projects were noted.
  • Management emphasized ongoing efforts to secure contracts and manage financial risks through refinancing and cost controls.

Final Takeaway

FTAI Infrastructure delivered a strong Q1 2025, with significant progress in EBITDA growth and strategic projects like Long Ridge consolidation and Repauno Phase 2. The company anticipates transformational growth in 2025, driven by new contracts, capacity enhancements, and data center development. Management’s confident outlook and disciplined financial strategy position the company for robust future performance.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Stellantis COO top contender for CEO job - Bloomberg
data.description:
Stellantis’ (NYSE:STLA) head of American operations is emerging as the top contender to succeed Carlos Tavares as the company’s search for a new chief executive enters its fifth month.

According to sources cited by Bloomberg, Antonio Filosa, Stellantis (NYSE:STLA) chief operating officer for the Americas and member of the interim executive committee, began his career at Fiat in 1999 and has been a member of Stellantis’ (NYSE:STLA) senior executive team since 2021 when Fiat, Chrysler Automotives merged with Group Peugeot S.A. to form Stellantis N.V. (STLA). He was appointed the company’s COO in October 2024.

Tavares was forced to resign in December of last year after his four-year tenure as CEO concluded with a turbulent final stretch.

data.title: American Strategic Investment targets portfolio diversification and debt reduction with Manhattan asset sales
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: American Strategic Investment Co. (NYC) Q1 2025

Management View

  • CEO Nicholas Schorsch highlighted that the company achieved 120 basis points of occupancy growth, reaching 82%. He noted active leasing engagements expected to close in the second quarter, which include both new and existing tenants.
  • The company is actively marketing 123 William Street and 196 Orchard for sale. Proceeds from these sales are planned to retire debt and diversify holdings into higher-yielding assets, aiming to enhance long-term value.
  • Schorsch emphasized that 51% of leases extend beyond 2030, supported by a tenant base where 77% are investment grade or implied investment grade.
  • CFO Michael LeSanto stated, "First quarter 2025 revenue was $12.3 million compared to $15.5 million in the first quarter of 2024," attributing the decrease to the sale of 9 Times Square in the previous quarter.
  • LeSanto further noted a GAAP net loss attributable to common stockholders of $8.6 million for Q1 2025, compared to $7.6 million in Q1 2024.

Outlook

  • Schorsch reiterated plans to enhance shareholder value by reducing leverage and reinvesting in higher-return opportunities. The divestiture strategy for Manhattan properties is expected to generate significant cash.
  • Management anticipates additional leasing agreements and renewals in Q2 2025, aligning with their tenant retention and value creation strategy.

Financial Results

  • Q1 2025 revenue was $12.3 million, down from $15.5 million in Q1 2024, primarily due to property sales.
  • The company recorded a GAAP net loss of $8.6 million in Q1 2025, greater than the $7.6 million loss in Q1 2024.
  • Adjusted EBITDA was negative $0.8 million compared to $2.9 million in Q1 2024. Cash net operating income was $4.2 million, down from $7 million in the same period last year.
  • Net leverage stood at approximately 58%, with a weighted average debt maturity of 2.3 years and a weighted average interest rate of 4.4%.

Q&A

  • NOT_APPLICABLE: No analyst questions or discussions were included in the provided transcript.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' sentiment: NOT_APPLICABLE due to absence of analyst questions.
  • Management maintained a positive tone during prepared remarks, emphasizing progress in leasing, strategic divestitures, and portfolio diversification. Schorsch expressed confidence in the company’s strategy, stating, "We believe this approach is key to enhancing shareholder value."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, the tone reflected continuity in strategic focus but underscored urgency in executing asset sales and generating returns.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remained consistent with the previous quarter, focusing on asset sales and diversification.
  • Occupancy improved from 80.8% in Q4 2024 to 82% in Q1 2025.
  • Revenue decreased sequentially due to the completed sale of 9 Times Square.
  • Leadership transition was completed with Nicholas Schorsch Jr. taking over as CEO, marking a shift in strategic emphasis.
  • Analysts' focus on leasing and divestiture strategy in Q4 2024 was absent in the current quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • The decline in revenue highlights reliance on property sales to maintain financial flexibility. Management aims to mitigate this by reinvesting in higher-yielding assets.
  • The short weighted average debt maturity of 2.3 years presents refinancing risks, particularly in a rising interest rate environment. Management emphasized focus on debt reduction to address this challenge.
  • Tenant retention and pipeline execution are critical to sustaining occupancy gains and revenue stability.

Final Takeaway

American Strategic Investment Co. is focused on executing its strategy of asset divestiture and portfolio diversification, targeting higher-yielding investments and leveraging gains to reduce debt. The Q1 2025 results underscore the importance of active leasing and strategic sales in driving long-term shareholder value. With 51% of leases extending beyond 2030 and a strong tenant base, the company remains positioned to navigate challenges while pursuing growth opportunities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: U.S. stocks lower ahead of possible weekend China trade deal
data.description:
Wall Street’s major averages are lower in Friday's early afternoon trade, a day after they rallied following a U.S.-U.K. trade agreement that renewed optimism over more potential economic and trade partnerships.

The benchmark S&P 500 (SP500) was last -0.1%, while the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was also -0.1%. The blue-chip Dow Jones (DJI) was last -0.3%.

Over the bond market, U.S. Treasury yields inched lower. The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) was down 2 basis points to 4.36%, while the 2-year Treasury yield (US2Y) was down 1 basis point to 3.87%.

Markets did not advance on Friday, despite closing higher on Thursday, after President Donald Trump announced the trade agreement with the United Kingdom. 

“U.S. consumers are worse off than in January, though better off than a week ago. Some U.K. exporters get better conditions, some U.S. exporters might get better conditions. Yesterday’s Bank of England's rate cut was a more important economic event,” UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

Probably more important for markets than the UK “deal” itself were Trump’s comments on China, saying that he expects “substantive” talks that could then yield tariff cuts, Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

Elsewhere on the tariff front, as Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and other U.S. officials head to Switzerland to start talks with high-level Chinese officials on trade, President Donald Trump suggested that reducing the current 145% tariff to 80% "seems right."

Furthermore, among Friday's top gainers are Insulet (PODD), 19.7%; Microchip Technology (MCHP), 10.8%; and Tesla (TSLA), 5.2%. 

Top decliners include Akamai Technologies (AKAM), -7.7%; Expedia (EXPE), -7.4%; and Crowdstrike Holdings (CRWD), -4.3%, after U.S. prosecutors and regulators probed a $32M deal between the company and Carahsoft.

data.title: Measles cases top 1K in the U.S. in 2025
data.description:
• The number of measles cases in the United States so far in 2025 has reached 1,001, according to the latest CDC data.
  • The last time the number topped 1K was in 2019, even as measles was declared eliminated in the U.S. in 2000.
  • Most of the cases -- around 800 -- started in West Texas with an outbreak there, but has now spread to New Mexico, Oklahoma and perhaps Kansas, according to CNN.
  • Measles cases this year have been reported in 31 states: Alaska, Arkansas, California, Colorado, Florida, Georgia, Hawaii, Illinois, Indiana, Kansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Maryland, Michigan, Minnesota, Missouri, Montana, New Jersey, New Mexico, New York City, New York State, North Dakota, Ohio, Oklahoma, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Tennessee, Texas, Vermont, Virginia, and Washington.
  • The CDC recommends the two-dose MMR vaccine to prevent the disease and reduce the disease severity in people exposed to the virus. Merck (NYSE:MRK) and GSK (NYSE:GSK) market MMR vaccines in the U.S.

data.title: Iberdrola to sell U.K. smart meter business to Macquarie in $1.2B deal
data.description:
Australian investment bank Macquarie (OTCPK:MCQEF) (OTCPK:MQBKY) said Friday it agreed to acquire Iberdrola's (OTCPK:IBDSF) (OTCPK:IBDRY) U.K. smart meter rental business, SP Smart Meters, for ~£900M ($1.19B).

Macquarie (OTCPK:MCQEF) (OTCPK:MQBKY) said the acquisition will add ~2.7M meters to its portfolio in the U.K., meaning it will manage more than 1 3M meters nationwide; it also has provided more than £1.5B of funding to assist with Britain's smart meter rollout.

By year-end 2024, 38M smart meters were installed in British homes and small businesses with support from the Smart Meter Rollout Program, led by the U.K. energy regulator Ofgem, as the country replaces traditional gas and electricity meters with smart meters.

Iberdrola (OTCPK:IBDSF) (OTCPK:IBDRY) said offloading non-core assets is key to funding growth and accelerating the clean energy transition; the sale is the second largest in its history, following the sale of its gas plants in Mexico last year.

data.title: Healthcare stocks that could be poised for a rebound
data.description:
Health care (NYSEARCA:XLV) is the most oversold global sector, according to BofA.

The sector was down 6.3% from its 200-day moving average in U.S. dollar terms, followed by energy (XLE) and biotechnology (XBI), -6.2% and -6.1% respectively.

The Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund ETF (NYSEARCA:XLV) is down 2.5% year-to-date, while the Vanguard Health Care Index Fund ETF Shares (VHT) is down 3.4% year-to-date.

Below are health care stocks that are oversold – measured by their relative strength index – but still rated “hold” or above by Seeking Alpha Quant metrics.

A stock is considered oversold when its Relative Strength Index (or RSI) dips below 30. This index measures the length of recent price changes to evaluate their “oversold” or “overbought” conditions. Stocks with an RSI below 30 may indicate that stocks are poised for a rebound.

Seeking Alpha's Quant system consistently outperforms the S&P 500 (SP500), driven by powerful computer processing and SA's special 'Quantamental' analysis. It awards grades to stocks based on collective value, growth, profitability, earnings per share revisions, and price momentum metrics.

data.title: Phathom Pharmaceuticals surges 50% following large insider purchase
data.description:
Phathom Pharmaceuticals has shot up ~50% in Friday trading after the disclosure that a member of its board made a significant purchase of shares.

Parikh Asit, who is also CEO of privately held MOMA Therapeutics, purchased 10K shares for a total value of ~$25K, according to a May 8 SEC filing. He now holds 95.5K shares total. 

Phathom's key marketed drug is Voquezna (vonoprazan) for heartburn related to non-erosive gastroesophageal reflux disease and erosive esophagitis.

Despite Friday's surge, Phathom is still down ~59% year to date.

The company's Q1 results, released earlier this month, missed on the bottom line and were in line with the top.

During the Q1 earnings call, CEO Steve Basta said he has four priorities for this year: growing sales of Voquezna; clarifying FDA exclusivity timeline; implementing cost-saving measures to achieve profitability in 2026; and leveraging Voquezna's potential to build a sustainable business.

Regarding exclusivity, the U.S. FDA's Orange Book states Voquezna has three exclusivity classifications. They are until: Nov. 1, 2026, as a new product; May 3, 2027, as a new chemical entity; and July 17, 2027, for the gastroesophageal reflux disease indication.

data.title: Sun Life stock gains after Q1 earnings beat, dividend boost
data.description:
Sun Life Financial (NYSE:SLF) stock climbed 4.1% in Friday midday trading after the company's Q1 earnings, released after Thursday's close, topped the average analyst estimate, and the life insurance firm boosted its dividend.

Earnings grew on a year-over-year basis across all its business. The company also increased its quarterly dividend to C$0.88 (US$0.63) per share from C$0.84.

Q1 underlying EPS of C$1.82 (US$1.31), beating the C$1.71 consensus estimate, grew from C$1.68 in Q4 2024 and C$1.50 in last year's Q1.

Underlying return on equity of 17.7% improved from 16.5% in the previous quarter and 16.0% a year ago.

"In an increasingly complex business environment, we continue to advance on our client impact strategy and strategic imperatives, underscored by new digital tools and capabilities, robust capital raising at SLC Management and strong sales and distribution in Asia," said President and CEO Kevin Strain.

Total underlying net insurance service pretax earnings jumped to C$837M in Q1 2025 from C$735M in Q4 2024 and C$712M in last year's Q1.

Total underlying net investment earnings, pretax, rose to C$422M from C$402M in the prior quarter and C$419M a year ago.

Underlying asset management earnings before taxes slipped to C$483M from C$505M in Q4 and increased from C$383M in Q1 2024.

Assets under management climbed to C$1.55T at March 31, 2025, from C$1.54T at Dec. 31, 2024, and C$1.47T at March 31, 2024.
